title: त्रिपुर सुन्दरी स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री त्रिपुर सुन्दरी स्तोत्रम् ॥

त्रिपुरसुन्दरीं वन्दे श्रीविद्या परदेवताम्।
महालक्ष्मीं महाकालीं महासरस्वतीं शुभाम्॥१॥

कादिविद्या महामन्त्रे षोडशाक्षरमालिनी।
श्रीचक्रराजनिलये नमस्ते त्रिपुरेश्वरी॥२॥

ललिता परमानन्दा राजराजेश्वरी शिवा।
कामेश्वरी कामकला कामाक्षी कामदायिनी॥३॥

सिन्दूरारुणवर्णाभा त्रिनेत्रा रत्नभूषणा।
पाशांकुशधनुर्बाणधारिणी जगदीश्वरी॥४॥

ब्रह्मोपेन्द्रमहेन्द्राद्यैः स्तुता सर्वसुरैः सदा।
त्रिपुरां ताम् नमस्यामि सुन्दरीं परमेश्वरीम्॥५॥

चिन्तामणिगृहान्तस्था कदम्बवनवासिनी।
सुधासागरमध्यस्था कामराजप्रियंवदा॥६॥

सर्वमंगलमांगल्ये शिवे सर्वार्थसाधिके।
शरण्ये त्र्यम्बके देवि नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥७॥

त्रिपुरसुन्दरीस्तोत्रं यः पठेद्भक्तिसंयुतः।
सौन्दर्यं लभते नित्यं सौभाग्यं चाश्नुते सदा॥८॥

॥ इति श्री त्रिपुर सुन्दरी स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Tripura Sundari Stotram ||

Tripura-sundarim Vande Shri-vidya Para-devatam.
Maha-lakshmim Maha-kalim Maha-sarasvatim Shubham.||1||

Kadi-vidya Maha-mantre Shodasha-akshara-malinim.
Shri-chakra-raja-nilaye Namaste Tripureshvari.||2||

Lalita Paramananda Raja-rajeshvari Shiva.
Kameshvari Kama-kala Kamakshi Kama-dayini.||3||

Sindura-aruna-varnabha Tri-netra Ratna-bhushana.
Pasha-ankusha-dhanur-bana-dharini Jagadishvari.||4||

Brahma-upendra-mahendra-adyaih Stuta Sarva-suraih Sada.
Tripuram Tam Namasyami Sundarim Parameshvarim.||5||

Chintamani-griha-antastha Kadamba-vana-vasinim.
Sudha-sagara-madhyastha Kama-raja-priyamvada.||6||

Sarva-mangala-mangalye Shive Sarvartha-sadhike.
Sharanye Tryambake Devi Narayani Namostu Te.||7||

Tripura-sundari-stotram Yah Pathed-bhakti-samyutah.
Saundaryam Labhate Nityam Saubhagyam Chash-nute Sada.||8||

|| Iti Shri Tripura Sundari Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: मैं श्रीविद्या की पर देवता त्रिपुरसुन्दरी को नमन करता हूँ, जो महालक्ष्मी, महाकाली और महासरस्वती हैं। षोडशाक्षर माला धारिणी, श्रीचक्रराज में निवास करने वाली त्रिपुरेश्वरी को नमस्कार। ललिता, परमानन्दा, राजराजेश्वरी, कामेश्वरी, कामाक्षी। सिन्दूर-अरुण वर्ण वाली, त्रिनेत्रा, पाशांकुश-धनुर्बाण धारिणी जगदीश्वरी। इस स्तोत्र को भक्ति से पढ़ने वाले को नित्य सौन्दर्य और सदा सौभाग्य प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Hymn to Tripura Sundari, the Supreme Goddess of Shri Vidya
Invokes the blessing of beauty, grace, and charm to the devotee
Invokes saubhagya (marital fortune) and all auspiciousness
Invokes the combined power of Lakshmi, Kali, and Saraswati
Sacred for Shri Vidya upasana and Lalita worship
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires through the grace of rajarajeshvari
description: Tripura Sundari Stotram (त्रिपुर सुन्दरी स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Goddess Parvati. Drawn from Brahmanda Purana, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to grant saubhagya (marital fortune) and all auspiciousness and bestow beauty, grace, and charm to the devotee. It is also said to fulfill all desires through the grace of Rajarajeshvari. This sacred text is ideally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Navratri and Lalita Panchami for enhanced blessings. The text contains 8 verses and takes around 5 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Tripura Sundari Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Tripura Sundari Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn to Lalita Tripurasundari for beauty, saubhagya, and Shri Vidya grace.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Tripura Sundari Stotram and who is the goddess Tripura Sundari?
seoFaqs.answer: The Tripura Sundari Stotram is an eight-verse Sanskrit hymn dedicated to Goddess Tripura Sundari (Lalita Tripurasundari, Rajarajeshvari, Shodashi, Bala), the supreme Devi of the Sri Vidya tradition and the third of the Dasha Mahavidya goddesses in Tantra. The name Tripura Sundari literally means the beautiful one of the three cities — interpreted variously as the ruler of the three lokas (heaven, earth, netherworld), the three states (waking, dreaming, deep sleep), the three gunas (sattva, rajas, tamas), or the three bodies (gross, subtle, causal). The stotram opens by identifying her as Sri Vidya Para-devata — the supreme goddess of Sri Vidya esoteric worship — and as the unified form of Maha-Lakshmi, Maha-Kali and Maha-Sarasvati. (Sources: Brahmanda Purana Lalitopakhyana; Tripura Rahasya; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is Sri Vidya and how is Tripura Sundari its central deity?
seoFaqs.answer: Sri Vidya is the most refined and esoteric school of goddess worship in Hindu Tantra, focused on the worship of Lalita Tripurasundari through the Sri Chakra yantra and the Panchadashi (15-syllable) and Shodashi (16-syllable) mantras. The tradition is divided into Kadi (beginning with the syllable Ka — favoured in Kashmir Shaiva and the Bhaskararaya school) and Hadi (beginning with Ha — favoured in Kerala) lineages. The stotram explicitly invokes both: Kadi-vidya Maha-mantre Shodashakshara-malini (verse 2). The Sri Chakra is understood as Tripurasundari's yantra-vigraha (geometric form), with the central bindu representing the goddess herself, surrounded by nine enclosures (avaranas) that map cosmology, body and mantra together. (Sources: Lalita Sahasranama; Bhaskararaya commentary; Hindupedia Sri Vidya.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Tripura Sundari described in the iconography of the stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 4 of the stotram paints her classical iconography: Sindura-aruna-varnabha (complexion of vermillion-red dawn), Trinetra (three-eyed), Ratna-bhushana (jewel-ornamented), Pasha-ankusha-dhanur-bana-dharini (wielding the noose, goad, sugarcane-bow and five flower-arrows). Verse 6 places her in Chintamani-griha (the wish-fulfilling jewel mansion), Kadamba-vana-vasini (resident of the kadamba forest), Sudha-sagara-madhyastha (seated in the midst of the ocean of nectar), and Kamaraja-priyamvada (sweet-speaking beloved of Kameshvara-Shiva). She is consort of Kameshwara (the Shiva form at the apex of the Sri Chakra), and is invoked under the famous epithets Lalita, Paramananda, Rajarajeshvari, Kameshwari, Kamakala and Kamakshi. (Sources: Lalita Sahasranama; Brahmanda Purana Lalitopakhyana; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Tripura Sundari Stotram be chanted for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: Friday (Shukravar) is the principal weekday — Friday is sacred to the Devi-tattva especially in her benevolent saubhagya-dayini form. Tuesday (Mangalvar) is also recommended for the fierce Tantric aspect. The stotram is especially chanted during Sharad Navratri (autumn nine-night festival, with the seventh, eighth and ninth days being most powerful for Sri Vidya), during Vasant Navratri (spring), on Lalita Panchami (Ashvina Shukla Panchami, the principal Lalita festival), on Lalita Jayanti (Magha Purnima or Magha Shukla Panchami in some traditions), on every Pournima especially Sharad Purnima, on Diwali, and during the Pratipat-Navami of every lunar fortnight by initiated Sri Vidya upasakas. Without formal Sri Vidya diksha however, lay devotees may chant the stotram daily as a stuti without entering the formal upasana. (Sources: Drikpanchang Lalita Panchami; Sri Vidya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Tripura Sundari Stotram promise to devotees?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing phalashruti (verse 8) declares Tripura-sundari-stotram yah pathed-bhakti-samyutah, saundaryam labhate nityam saubhagyam chashnute sada — the devotee who recites this stotram with devotion obtains lasting beauty (saundarya) and ever-enjoys saubhagya (auspicious marital fortune). Tradition extends the benefits to: removal of marriage delays for unmarried women, conjugal harmony, beauty and grace (since Sundari literally means the beautiful one), wealth (the combined power of Maha-Lakshmi), wisdom (Maha-Sarasvati), and protection (Maha-Kali); fulfilment of all desires through Rajarajeshvari's universal sovereignty; and ultimately moksha through Sri Vidya upasana since Tripurasundari at her highest aspect is the para-shakti, the unmanifest source of Brahman itself. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi phalashruti; Lalita Sahasranama Phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Tripura Sundari Stotram differ from Lalita Sahasranama and Tripura Sundari Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: All three are Lalita-Tripurasundari recitations of varying length and depth. The Tripura Sundari Stotram (8 verses) is a brief praise hymn used as a daily entry-point recitation, suited for lay devotees and Sri Vidya aspirants. The Lalita Sahasranama (1000 names from the Brahmanda Purana Lalitopakhyana) is the principal scripture of Sri Vidya, read in saptaha-parayana, and forms the basis of formal Lalita archana with kumkum and pushpa offerings. The Tripura Sundari Sahasranama (a separate composition listing 1000 names with a slightly different selection and arrangement, found in some Tantric collections) is used for elaborate Sri Chakra puja by initiated upasakas. The Khadgamala Stotram lists the names of all the deities of the Sri Chakra avaranas in a yantra-walk through the chakra. The eight-verse stotram is the accessible starting point. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi Sri Vidya collection; Lalita Sahasranama; Tantric tradition.)
